archetype: ARCHETYPE-2 (Integration) for BOTH `@netscript/auth-better-auth` and `@netscript/auth-workos` — each wraps exactly one external identity system behind the merged `AuthenticatorPort` (no new public port; ports are CONSUMED from `@netscript/service/auth`). ARCHETYPE-5 (schema-contribution) overlay for `@netscript/auth-better-auth` ONLY (better-auth Prisma model generation routed through NetScript's Prisma flow). Precedent: `packages/prisma-adapter-mysql/` (standalone Archetype-2 adapter package) + `packages/queue` (port + factory + lazy adapter import).
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (both packages target the @netscript/service composition root via withAuthn injection + the Hono request pipeline: mount helper, Set-Cookie emission through AuthnResult.setCookies)
lockedDecisions: Ports stay upstream: both packages CONSUME AuthenticatorPort/AuthorizerPort/Principal/AuthnRequest/AuthnResult from @netscript/service/auth (merged #77 @ 79f5840d); neither redefines them. No @netscript/service public-surface change.

Instance injection (consumer-brings-the-instance): createBetterAuthAuthenticator({ auth }) and createWorkosAuthenticator({ workos, cookiePassword }). The packages are thin mapping/compat layers, mirroring createRedisQueue(url) / the Prisma driver adapter.

better-auth tier = verify + storage. Storage WRAPS better-auth's OWN first-party prismaAdapter (better-auth/adapters/prisma) over the consumer's @netscript/database client (research F1 simplification — wrap, don't reinvent a Convex-createApi CRUD store). Unlocks orgs/admin/api-keys/2FA/persistent sessions.

WorkOS tier = verification-first: loadSealedSession({ sessionData, cookiePassword }).authenticate() -> Principal; optional separate JWKS access-token factory. Webhook->DB org/user sync DEFERRED (debt-logged optional fast-follow).

Set-Cookie / refresh-on-read channel = AuthnResult.setCookies (+ responseHeaders) per the merged seam (resolves Q4). Adapters with scheme:'custom' and provider identity in Principal.claims (resolves Q3); claim keys follow the shipped seam example (organizationId, sessionId) plus raw provider claims.

Catalog law: add better-auth ^1.6.20 + @workos-inc/node ^10.4.0 to the catalog, declared ONLY in their own packages via catalog:, never in core @netscript/service. @better-auth/cli is dev/tooling (invoked via a .llm/tools/ script), not a runtime catalog entry. Confirm 'latest' via deno task deps:latest AFTER entries exist — never deno outdated --latest.

NOT an e2e-cli-gate slice: no @netscript/cli touch, no scaffold-output change this slice -> do NOT attach e2e-cli-gate label or run scaffold.runtime smoke. A CLI auth-provider scaffold prompt is a deferred fast-follow.
better-auth Prisma models are GENERATED via @better-auth/cli (no auto-migration); the slice ships generation + wiring + docs, the CONSUMER owns running the migration.
contracts: AuthenticatorPort / AuthnRequest (header/headers()/cookie()/method/path) / AuthnResult ({ ok, principal, responseHeaders?, setCookies? } | { ok:false, reason }) / Principal (subject/scopes/roles/scheme/claims) — REUSED unchanged from @netscript/service/auth; the two adapters are new external implementors.
@netscript/auth-workos: createWorkosAuthenticator({ workos, cookiePassword }): AuthenticatorPort + optional createWorkosAccessTokenAuthenticator({ clientId, jwksUrl? }). New package surface.

@netscript/auth-better-auth: createNetscriptBetterAuth({ prisma, provider, ...betterAuthOptions }) (wraps prismaAdapter), createBetterAuthAuthenticator({ auth }): AuthenticatorPort, mountBetterAuthHandler(app, auth, { basePath }). New package surface.
Catalog: better-auth ^1.6.20 + @workos-inc/node ^10.4.0 (new entries, package-local usage only).
better-auth generated Prisma models routed through NetScript's Prisma/migration flow (Archetype-5 schema-contribution analogue) — generation tool surface, not runtime.
testPlan: unit @netscript/auth-workos: sealed-session authenticate() -> mapped Principal (subject/scopes/roles/claims); authenticated:false -> { ok:false }; rotated session -> setCookies populated. Mocked WorkOS client.
unit @netscript/auth-workos access-token: JWKS bearer verify -> Principal (mocked JWKS / jose).
Deno node-compat smoke: @workos-inc/node@10 (Node>=22.11) loadSealedSession/.authenticate() resolve under Deno 2.8 (jose/iron-webcrypto/uint8array-extras).
unit @netscript/auth-better-auth: createBetterAuthAuthenticator getSession({ headers }) -> Principal mapping; reject path; refresh-on-read rotated cookie -> setCookies. Mocked betterAuth instance.

integration @netscript/auth-better-auth storage: createNetscriptBetterAuth over a Prisma test client (SQLite/pglite or transaction-rollback fixture) round-trips a session/user; prismaAdapter wired correctly.
mount helper: mountBetterAuthHandler routes /api/auth/** on a Hono app and the auth routes are exempt from the authenticator (allowAnonymous).
JSR readiness: publish:dry-run for both packages + doc-lint over the FULL export map of each (not mod.ts alone).
planGateSelfCheck: Research present and current: PASS — plan grounded against the MERGED #77 seam (packages/service/src/auth/types.ts @ 79f5840d) re-read directly; AuthnResult.setCookies, AuthnRequest.headers()/cookie(), Principal scheme/claims confirmed (not assumed). Decisions locked: PASS — 9 locked decisions with rationale (separate packages, ports-upstream, instance injection, better-auth verify+storage wrapping native prismaAdapter, WorkOS verify-first, setCookies channel, catalog law, non-e2e-gate, generation-not-migration). Open-decision sweep: PASS — Q3/Q4/Q5 all resolved against the merged seam; remaining risks (Deno node-compat, provider API drift, migration ownership) are RISKS with mitigations, not open decisions. Commit slices: PASS — 6 ordered slices, each names files + gate. Risk register: PASS — node-compat, better-auth API drift, storage-migration ownership. Gate set: PASS — ARCHETYPE-2 + ARCHETYPE-5 overlay + SCOPE-service, scoped check/lint/fmt wrappers, per-package test + publish:dry-run + doc-lint full-export + arch:check + deps:latest/audit; e2e:cli explicitly EXCLUDED with rationale. jsr-audit: PASS — applied to both new package surfaces. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: LOW-MEDIUM — most likely challenge is whether the better-auth storage tier belongs in THIS slice vs a fast-follow (scope size) and Deno node-compat feasibility for the provider SDKs; plan offers explicit rescope fallbacks in §7.
openQuestions: RESOLVED (merged seam): Q3 Principal mapping -> scheme:'custom' + claims bag (organizationId/sessionId + raw claims). Q4 refresh -> AuthnResult.setCookies. Q5 handler mount -> mountBetterAuthHandler + allowAnonymous.

RESCOPE-CANDIDATE for PLAN-EVAL: better-auth storage tier (slices 4-5) materially enlarges the slice; PLAN-EVAL may split storage+schema into a fast-follow leaving verify-only in this slice. Plan keeps it together (matches the locked 'most better-auth features' bar) but flags the split as an acceptable rescope.

VERIFY-ITEM (not a decision): Deno 2.8 node-compat for @workos-inc/node@10 and better-auth@1.6 — a slice-2/slice-4 gate; if incompatible, drift + rescope WorkOS to JWKS-only / surface the better-auth limitation.
risks: Deno node-compat for @workos-inc/node@10 (Node>=22.11) and better-auth@1.6 under Deno 2.8; mitigated by an explicit node-compat smoke gate + JWKS-only fallback for WorkOS.
better-auth API drift (auth.api.getSession / prismaAdapter import path) pinned to 1.6.20; mitigated by citing the version so IMPL re-verifies against the installed surface, not docs.
Storage-migration ownership ambiguity (slice ships generation+wiring, consumer runs migration); mitigated by explicit boundary docs so it is not read as auto-migration.
Scope size: verify + storage + schema + mount across two packages is large; mitigated by 6 commit-by-slice gates and a documented storage/verify split as an acceptable PLAN-EVAL rescope.
